headlines.title: Former Nato chief warns UK security 'in peril' as he accuses Starmer of 'corrosive complacency'
headlines.description: Lord George Robertson, a former Labour defence secretary, will use a speech to accuse "non-military experts in the Treasury" of "vandalism".

headlines.title: US blockade of Iranian ports explained in two minutes
headlines.description: The BBC's security correspondent Frank Gardner explains how the US blockade of Iranian ports will work.

headlines.title: Watchdog investigates 11 police officers over handling of Wimbledon school crash
headlines.description: The families of Nuria Sajjad and Selena Lau, who died at an end-of-term tea party, say the initial investigation was flawed.

headlines.title: Greek police using masked migrants to forcibly push other migrants back across border
headlines.description: Mercenaries recruited since at least 2020 for illegal migrant ‘pushbacks’ in Greece, BBC told

headlines.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first
headlines.description: The Lebanese government go into peace talks with limited influence over the group.

headlines.title: Harry and Meghan mix charity and business on Australia visit
headlines.description: There are questions over whether local taxpayers are picking up any of the bill for policing the visit.

headlines.title: Why doctors' strikes can actually lead to shorter waiting times and what it costs
headlines.description: Some hospital trusts tell the BBC previous action has seen shorter waits, faster decisions and calmer corridors.

headlines.title: Chris Mason: How Lammy and Vance's unlikely friendship is being utilised
headlines.description: Their relations remain warm just as those between their leaders seems decidedly chilly, Chris Mason writes.

headlines.title: Oasis among record number of British acts entering Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
headlines.description: Sade, Phil Collins, Billy Idol and Joy Division/New Order are also among the recipients.

headlines.title: Jorginho withdraws criticism of Chappell Roan
headlines.description: Former Chelsea and Arsenal player Jorginho issues a statement saying pop singer Chappell Roan was not involved in an incident in which a security guard reduced his daughter to tears.

headlines.title: Titanic rescue hero's gold watch could go for £100k
headlines.description: The item was awarded to John Richardson for his efforts in rescuing survivors of the 1912 disaster.

headlines.title: Oil prices ease on hopes of new US-Iran peace talks
headlines.description: Crude prices fall back below $100 a barrel as markets hope an agreement can be reached between the two sides.

headlines.title: BBC joins paramedics on duty in Lebanon after Israeli strikes
headlines.description: BBC Middle East correspondent Hugo Bachega joins paramedics on duty in Nabatieh - a city that was once vibrant, but is now abandoned.

headlines.title: This blockade is a gamble. Will it work?
headlines.description: The US military is gambling on the blockade denying Iran vital trade - but questions remain on whether the bet will pay off.

headlines.title: Israelis war-weary but most oppose Iran ceasefire, poll suggests
headlines.description: The vast majority did not believe Hezbollah or Iran had been severely weakened by weeks of Israeli and US attacks, according to the poll.

headlines.title: Tracking the ships crossing the Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: The first vessel has crossed the Strait of Hormuz after the US imposed a military blockade of Iran's ports.

headlines.title: Adam Peaty on his return to the pool, LA 2028 and Gordon Ramsay's wedding speech
headlines.description: Two-time Olympic champion Adam Peaty discusses his latest return, his path to the LA Olympics and Gordon Ramsay's wedding speech.

headlines.title: Why one school has banned phones for some pupils - but not others
headlines.description: Schools across Wales say phone bans improve behaviour but can mean conflict with pupils and parents.

headlines.title: I was minutes from dying - then I heard the lifeboat crewman's voice
headlines.description: A woman is reunited with the lifeboat crew who saved her life after her paddleboard was swept out to sea

headlines.title: How Trump’s Jesus-like image and feud with the Pope are sparking backlash
headlines.description: BBC's Sarah Smith looks at how the president's most ardent supporters are reacting to his latest social media posts.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'They didn't have to die' and 'You're not the Messiah'
headlines.description: Southport murder inquiry findings and Trump deletes Jesus-like AI image lead Tuesday's papers.

headlines.title: Hospital at centre of child HIV outbreak caught reusing syringes in undercover filming
headlines.description: Footage shows staff in Pakistan injecting without gloves and reusing syringes, but the hospital boss refuses to acknowledge it is genuine.

headlines.title: Wins and challenges: Zohran Mamdani's first 100 days in office
headlines.description: Mamdani has made progress on pledges for free child care and city-owned grocery stores, but many goals remain.

headlines.title: Al Fayed enablers 'must face accountability', survivors say
headlines.description: Two survivors want to see those who enabled Al Fayed to carry out his abuse to face justice.

headlines.title: Founder of China's Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud
headlines.description: Evergrande was once China's biggest real estate firm, with a stock market valuation of more than $50bn.

headlines.title: Hungary's next PM would pick up if Putin calls and tell him to stop Ukraine war
headlines.description: Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations, but Péter Magyar says he will not call Vladimir Putin himself.

headlines.title: Irish musician Moya Brennan dies aged 73
headlines.description: The singer came to prominence when she began performing with her family in the group Clannad, which was formed in 1970.

headlines.title: Hollywood stars unite to oppose Paramount and Warner Bros Discovery merger
headlines.description: Stars like Emma Thompson and Ben Stiller signed an open letter against the deal, which Paramount said will ensure creators "have more avenues for their work, not fewer".

headlines.title: South Korea jails 90-year-old woman for laundering son's drug money
headlines.description: The woman has been sentenced to a year in jail for laundering money for her son who is imprisoned in Cambodia.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Americanswers... on 5 Live! Donald Trump vs Pope Leo on Iran
headlines.description: The US president insults the pope and depicts himself as Jesus

headlines.title: The "Systemic Failures" Before The Southport Attack
headlines.description: Inquiry finds Southport killer's family and authorities could have prevented attack.

headlines.title: Jeers and defensive crisis - Carrick facing first big Man Utd test
headlines.description: Manchester United head coach Michael Carrick has problems to solve before his side's crucial trip to Chelsea on Saturday evening.

headlines.title: Carrick says Martinez hair-pull red card 'one of worst decisions I've seen'
headlines.description: Manchester United defender Lisandro Martinez is sent off after 56 minutes for pulling the hair of Leeds United striker Dominic Calvert-Lewin at Old Trafford.

headlines.title: Stokes says he must work differently with McCullum
headlines.description: England captain Ben Stokes says suggestions of a rift with Brendon McCullum have been overstated, but acknowledges he must work with the head coach in a "slightly different way".

headlines.title: 'I should have died' - Taylor on 10 years since forced retirement
headlines.description: Speaking 10 years on from his retirement aged 26, former England batter James Taylor speaks about how he "should have died" from his heart condition.

headlines.title: The Briton who remains in demand in NBA at 74
headlines.description: BBC Sport charts the remarkable career of Alex McKechnie, who left Scotland to become one of the NBA's top physiotherapists with a reputation of saving basketball careers.

headlines.title: How Champions League could decide £100m Alvarez's future
headlines.description: With Julian Alvarez's Atletico Madrid future uncertain, European football expert Guillem Balague looks at how pivotal their Champions League run could prove.
